opp.ais_why:
• On 9 Feb 2026, the rep's Discovery Call summary records Zycus's positioning: 'Zycus also has stronger automation, AI co-pilot functionality & Agents.' This is a Zycus-side positioning sentence inserted into the discovery resolution narrative ‚Äî NOT a verbatim buyer-side statement. EQUATE itself is positioning the deal as 'Want to start with a small, budget-sensitive scope focused on sourcing,' not AI.
• On 30 Mar 2026: 'Demo went well, next step is to do a light RFI amongst the competitors ahead of a formal RFP - not 100% clear whether it will go beyond iSource/iSupply in this phase, have made clear that we have commercial minimums to consider.' EQUATE situation as captured: 'EQUATE is a large Kuwaiti Petrochem co & uses SAP ERP (moving to S/4HANA in Feb), Has been on SAP Ariba for ~10 years, using only RFI/RFQ/RFP and reverse auctions, Most post-bid work handled offline/manually... Low team adoption: 52 licensed users but only ~10 actively using Ariba.' Buyer ask is usability and S/4HANA integration.
• Confidence is Medium. Product scope is iSource;iSupplier only (no AI SKUs). The rep used 'AI co-pilot functionality & Agents' as a Zycus differentiator vs Ariba but EQUATE's stated needs are usability, large-document handling, and S/4HANA integration ‚Äî not AI. The single 'AI' keyword in NextStep is Zycus positioning, not buyer pull. Score 4 reflects rep-led AI positioning to a buyer who hasn't asked for it.

competition.narrative: EQUATE is evaluating Zycus against SAP Ariba (10-year incumbent), GEP Worldwide, iValua, and JAGGAER in a lightweight competitive RFI process. SAP Ariba is the primary competitive threat and the path of least resistance: it is both the incumbent solution (10 years, already integrated, known by the team) and the strategic ERP vendor whose S/4HANA platform went live in Feb 2026, creating vendor lock-in and political inertia. The buyer explicitly stated on 30 Mar that the 'next step is to do a light RFI amongst the competitors ahead of a formal RFP' (Next_Step 30 Mar), confirming GEP, iValua, JAGGAER, and Ariba are all in the mix. The do-nothing risk (stay with Ariba) is high given the budget gap and lack of executive sponsorship. How we win: position Zycus as the usability and AI leader with mature S/4HANA integration (faster, lower-risk implementation than competitors), stronger automation and Merlin agentic capabilities, large-document handling and legacy-data migration expertise, and a modular platform that lets EQUATE start small and scale (addressing the budget-sensitivity concern). The wedge is AI-driven procurement intelligence and user adoption vs Ariba's legacy UX, activated through an ROI-backed pilot proposal and Economic Buyer engagement to overcome the budget gap and vendor lock-in.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's decision criteria are well-documented from the discovery call and RFI response, centered on usability vs Ariba, large-file handling, and data migration capability. EQUATE stated they want (1) a user-friendly, clean, simple interface with better adoption than Ariba (only 10 of 52 users active), (2) large document/file capacity to handle engineering drawings and plant construction documents, (3) expertise and a plan for legacy data migration (supplier onboarding and bid history from 10 years on Ariba), and (4) clarity on implementation approach (out-of-the-box vs custom) and cloud/hosting model (Next_Step 9 Feb). The light RFI submitted 13 May was scoped to eSourcing only, but Zycus raised a quotation for Intake + iSupplier + iSource to test the buyer's reaction and meet commercial minimums (Next_Step 13 May). The buyer's stated concern is that inability to support large engineering drawings or migrate history would block switching (Next_Step 9 Feb). Zycus positioned usability/UX improvement, stronger automation and AI agents, mature S/4HANA integration, large document handling, and modular platform scalability as the resolution (Next_Step 9 Feb positioning). The criteria are confirmed; the gap is that the buyer has not yet validated these capabilities in a structured evaluation (no use-case demos, workshops, or test cases beyond the 30 Mar demo) and has gone silent before advancing to formal RFP.

hard.ais_why:
• On 9 Feb 2026, the rep's Discovery Call summary records Zycus's positioning: 'Zycus also has stronger automation, AI co-pilot functionality & Agents.' This is a Zycus-side positioning sentence inserted into the discovery resolution narrative ‚Äî NOT a verbatim buyer-side statement. • On 30 Mar 2026: 'Demo went well, next step is to do a light RFI amongst the competitors ahead of a formal RFP - not 100% clear whether it will go beyond iSource/iSupply in this phase, have made clear that we have commercial minimums to consider.' EQUATE situation as captured: 'EQUATE is a large Kuwaiti Petrochem co & uses SAP ERP (moving to S/4HANA in Feb), Has been on SAP Ariba for ~10 years, using only RFI/RFQ/RFP and reverse auctions, Most post-bid work handled offline/manually... Low team adoption: 52 licensed users but only ~10 actively using Ariba.' Buyer ask is usability and S/4HANA integration.
• Confidence is Medium. Product scope is iSource;iSupplier only (no AI SKUs). The rep used 'AI co-pilot functionality & Agents' as a Zycus differentiator vs Ariba but EQUATE's stated needs are usability, large-document handling, and S/4HANA integration ‚Äî not AI. The single 'AI' keyword in NextStep is Zycus positioning, not buyer pull. Score 4 reflects rep-led AI positioning to a buyer who hasn't asked for it.

Situation

•EQUATE is a large Kuwaiti Petrochem co & uses SAP ERP (moving to S/4HANA in Feb) ,

•Has been on SAP Ariba for ~10 years, using only RFI/RFQ/RFP and reverse auctions,

•Most post‑bid work handled offline/manually,

•Exploring alternatives to Ariba after a positive Gartner mention of Zycus,

•Want to start with a small, budget‑sensitive scope focused on sourcing.\xa0

Impact

•Heavy offline workload: proposals downloaded, shared, evaluated, awarded and approved manually outside the system,

•Current eSourcing process is not fully digitalized,

•Low team adoption: 52 licensed users but only ~10 actively using Ariba;

•Want better usability and a tool the whole team will use,

•Concern about document/file limits and legacy data migration (supplier onboarding & bid history); inability to support large engineering drawings or migrate history would block switching.\xa0

(Positioning) Resolution

• Zycus platform (incl eSourcing/Supplier Management) usability/UX significant improvement versus Ariba,

•Zycus also has stronger automation, AI co-pilot functionality & Agents,

•Zycus has strong/mature S/4HANA integration, for faster, lower risk, lower TCO implementation

•Zycus supports Large document handling and legacy data migration capability.

•Zycus is a natively integrated Modular platform, start now where the budget permits, grow and add more functionality later
